entries.statement: Define and maintain the enterprise AI governance framework, including the AI system inventory, risk tiering, review thresholds, and decommissioning criteria.

Establish and maintain the enterprise AI inventory, cataloging all AI systems in production with risk tier, owner, training data provenance, model version, and review status, ensuring all applications are registered and subject to appropriate review and monitoring.
Lead enterprise AI ethics reviews for high-stakes applications, including impact assessments, bias audits, and explainability documentation prior to production deployment.
Define the enterprise AI acceptable use policy, prohibited use categories, and human oversight requirements for AI-assisted decision making.
Conduct AI impact assessments for new and materially modified AI systems prior to production deployment, producing documented risk findings with recommended mitigations and residual risk statements.
Manage ongoing AI policy compliance monitoring, including periodic review of deployed AI systems against current policy requirements and regulatory obligations.
Execute bias audits on AI systems in development and production, documenting methodology, findings, and recommended interventions.
Develop and maintain responsible AI assessment tools, templates, and rubrics for use across the enterprise AI development lifecycle.
Monitor AI regulatory developments and assess their implications for the enterprise AI system portfolio, producing compliance gap analyses for impacted systems.
Operate AI governance review processes, including intake triage, stakeholder coordination, assessment scheduling, decision documentation, and escalation to executive governance bodies.

Conduct iterative AI risk assessment across the system lifecycle, including risk identification at inception, development-stage risk updates, deployment readiness evaluation, and post-deployment risk reassessment triggered by material changes or emerging risk signals.

Produce regulatory-grade AI system documentation, including technical specifications, risk management records, conformity assessment evidence, and instructions-for-use documentation meeting applicable regulatory standards.
Design and operate AI post-market monitoring systems, including performance surveillance, risk signal identification, deployer data integration, and trigger criteria for corrective action.
Manage AI system incidents, including serious incident identification, root cause analysis, corrective action implementation, and mandatory regulatory reporting under applicable AI regulation.

Assess and monitor third-party AI risks, including vendor evaluation, pre-trained model provenance review, dependency tracking, contract management for AI components, and contingency planning for service disruption.
Govern AI testing, evaluation, verification, and validation processes, including test protocol approval, methodology assessment, results review, and TEVV process effectiveness evaluation.
Conduct fundamental rights impact assessments for AI systems, including rights identification, disproportionate impact analysis, and integration of assessment findings into deployment decisions.

Design and deliver AI governance literacy and training programs, including competency assessment, role-based curriculum development, and evaluation of training effectiveness in building organizational AI governance fluency.

Establish and operate stakeholder engagement and feedback mechanisms for AI governance, including affected community identification, external feedback collection, adjudication of conflicting input, and integration of stakeholder perspectives into governance decisions.

Execute AI conformity assessment procedures, including selection and management of assessment pathways, notified body engagement, CE marking documentation, and post-assessment surveillance compliance.

Implement AI transparency and disclosure requirements, including interaction notification mechanisms, synthetic content identification, AI-generated content labeling, and transparency measures appropriate to different regulatory contexts.

Design and maintain AI risk quantification and measurement approaches, including risk metric selection, proxy indicator development for unmeasurable risks, fairness measurement across protected attributes, and translation of measurement results into governance actions.

Govern AI system decommissioning, including safe phase-out procedures, risk assessment during transition, data retention decisions, stakeholder notification, and documentation of decommissioning rationale and outcomes.
